headline: How The Cerence (CRNC) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Reset Fair Value To US$10
summary: Cerence’s fair value estimate has been trimmed from US$12.50 to US$10.00, a roughly 20% cut that reflects an updated view of the story. Recent Street research has also moved closer to that US$10.00 level, as several firms reset their price targets around concerns about revenue growth and execution. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets fit into the broader analyst narrative and what to watch in order to keep up with it. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full...

headline: How The Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE:BABA) Narrative Is Shifting With AI And Cloud Uncertainty
summary: Alibaba Group Holding’s updated analyst fair value estimate has shifted slightly, moving from US$198.28 to US$199.61 per share. Analysts linking this change to fresh research are split, with some pointing to potential upside if cloud and AI investments translate into earnings, while others highlight balance sheet and margin questions that temper enthusiasm. As you read on, you will see how these differing views shape the evolving narrative and what signals to watch next. Analyst Price Targets...

headline: Goldman Hikes Oil Price Forecast Again
summary: Goldman Sachs raised its oil-price forecast for the second time in just over a week, anticipating a longer disruption of flows through the all-important Strait of Hormuz.  Commodity strategists now expect Brent crude oil futures to average $98 a barrel in March and April, up around 40% from its average price in 2025.  The strategists, led by Daan Struyven, expect prices to fall back to $71 by the fourth quarter—still higher than the roughly $60 at which Brent finished last year.

The strategists, led by Daan Struyven, expect prices to fall back to $71 by the fourth quarter—still higher than the roughly $60 at which Brent finished last year.

headline: Goldman Sachs Revises Fed Rate Cut Forecast to September as Iran War Threatens Inflation
summary: Wall Street giant Goldman Sachs has revised its forecast on when the U.S. Federal Reserve will make the first rate cut this year. This delay in their forecast for the first Fed rate cut this year comes as the Iran war threatens to drive inflation higher, which could make the U.S. Central Bank more wary
